title: Standalone Cloud-Served License
text: Standalone Cloud-Served License A standalone cloud-served license is used for any Uniface application where the license is locked to a machine, and connects to the Sentinel Cloud Server (SCC) to obtain and periodically renew the lease on the license. This is also referred to as lease mode . Cloud-served licensing requires an internet connection to activate the license and to periodically renew the lease on the license by connecting to the SCC. If the application has no internet connection, it can still continue to run as long as the lease period has not expired. For the Uniface 10 Community Edition, only standalone cloud-served licenses are used. For more information on how to activate your license for the Community Edition, see Install the Uniface Community Edition . For the Enterprise Edition: To configure a standalone cloud-served license, see Configuring Standalone Cloud-Served Licenses . To return a standalone cloud-served license, see Returning Standalone Cloud-Served Licenses . To update a standalone cloud-served license, see Updating Standalone Cloud-Served Licenses . Note: For the Enterprise Edition, cloud-served licensing is only possible on Windows and Linux. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
